text:
The automated checker's "author mismatch" (citing "C. Wick" and "Informatik-Spektrum") is clearly a case of the checker having matched a different, unrelated paper that happens to share the title "Deep Learning" — a short review article published in the German journal *Informatik-Spektrum* by C. Wick, not the famous textbook. The cited reference itself is definitively verified by multiple authoritative sources including the book's own official website (deeplearningbook.org), MIT Press, Google Books, ACM Digital Library, and Amazon, all confirming the exact title, authors, publisher, and year.

EXPLANATION: The cited book *Deep Learning* by Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, and Aaron Courville, published by MIT Press in 2016, is a well-known, widely verified textbook with its own official page at deeplearningbook.org and confirmed across MIT Press, ACM Digital Library, and Google Books. The automated checker's author/venue mismatch is due to it incorrectly matching a different paper (a short German journal article by C. Wick) with the same generic title.
LINK: https://www.deeplearningbook.org/
FOUND_TITLE: Deep Learning
FOUND_AUTHORS: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville
FOUND_YEAR: 2016
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
